channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ukraine says more than 150 Chinese mercenaries are fighting for Russia in Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Wednesday expanded on its claim that significant numbers of Chinese nationals are fighting for Russia’s invading army, saying it had gathered detailed intelligence on more than 150 mercenaries Moscow allegedly recruited through social media.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israeli strike on Gaza apartment building kills at least 23, officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli aircraft struck a residential block in war-ravaged northern Gaza on Wednesday, killing at least 23 people, health officials said, as the renewed fighting in the devastated Palestinian enclave showed no signs of slowing.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China issues travel advisory for US
item.description: China issued a travel advisory Wednesday for citizens visiting the U.S. The warning comes as a trade war has escalated between the nations, and as more scrutiny is being put on foreign nationals holding visas in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: US warns China intelligence targeting fired federal workers
item.description: The Trump administration is warning federal workers about efforts by Chinese intelligence to target current and former U.S. officials for recruitment.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Trump hits back with a 125% tariff in escalating trade war with China
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — President Donald Trump raised the tariff on Chinese imports to 125% on Wednesday, hours after China boosted the duty on American goods to 84% in an escalating battle that threatens to disrupt trade between the world's two largest economies.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mike Huckabee confirmed as Trump's ambassador to Israel
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday confirmed former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee (R) as President Trump’s ambassador to Israel. The upper chamber largely voted along party lines, with 53 in favor and 46 against. Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) voted in favor of the nomination.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Iran hit with new sanctions ahead of nuclear talks with US
item.description: The United States imposed new sanctions on Iran on Wednesday, days before the Islamic republic’s scheduled talks with Washington this weekend in Oman.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Americans convicted in Congo of a botched coup attempt now face US charges
item.description: Three Americans repatriated to the United States from Congo have been charged by the U.S. Justice Department with staging an elaborate coup attempt aimed at overthrowing the African nation’s government.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Schoenbaum And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope Francis meets privately with King Charles and Queen Camilla during his Vatican convalescence
item.description: Pope Francis met privately with King Charles III and Queen Camilla at the Vatican during the royal couple’s four-day state visit to Italy and on the occasion of their 20th wedding anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Giada Zampano And Colleen Barry, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: At least 124 dead after roof collapse at Dominican club, officials scramble to ID victims
item.description: Rescue crews from Puerto Rico and Israel arrived Wednesday to help with the search. Officials said crews used sonar to detect what could be one person still breathing under the rubble.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump restoring some foreign aid programs
item.description: The Trump administration on Tuesday claimed foreign aid programs that were cut in error would be restored.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Nicaragua’s crackdown on the Catholic Church is worsening, exiles and human rights advocates say
item.description: Preparations for popular, often daylong Lent and Holy Week processions are underway across Latin America — but not in Nicaragua. They’ve been largely banned for a second year, one of many concerns for the faithful in a country that human rights advocates, exiled priests and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's president insists Tehran does not seek a nuclear bomb and dangles US business opportunities
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — Iran's president again pledged Wednesday that his nation is “not after a nuclear bomb” ahead of talks between Tehran and the United States, going as far as dangling the prospect of direct American investment in the Islamic Republic if the countries can reach a deal.
item.dc:creator: NASSER KARIMI and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: O’Leary advocating for 400 percent tariffs on China
item.description: "Shark Tank" investor Kevin O’Leary said Tuesday that he is an advocate for placing 400 percent tariffs on China as the Trump administration ramps up its import taxes on the country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Long, fraught timeline of tensions between Iran and the US as nuclear negotiations approach
item.description: Iran and the United States will hold talks in the sultanate of Oman on Saturday in an attempt to jump-start negotiations over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program. President Donald Trump insists they’ll be direct negotiations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US restores urgent food aid, except in Afghanistan and Yemen, two of the world’s poorest countries
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's administration has reversed cuts in emergency food aid to several nations but maintained them in Afghanistan and Yemen, two of the world’s poorest countries.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy, Riazat Butt And Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 8 people die from cholera in South Sudan as funding cuts force longer walks to clinics
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Five children were among eight people who died from cholera in South Sudan after aid cuts are forcing patients to walk for hours to the nearest clinics, an aid organization said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Korea's military fires warning shots after North Korean soldiers cross the border
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's military fired warning shots after North Korean soldiers crossed the rivals' tense border on Tuesday, South Korean officials said, the first known border intrusion by North Korea in nearly a year.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US admiral at NATO fired in expanding national security purge
item.description: U.S. Navy Vice Admiral Shoshana Chatfield is one of only a handful of female Navy three-star officers.
item.dc:creator: Phil Stewart, Idrees Ali, Lili Bayer / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills a journalist in a media tent outside a Gaza hospital
item.description: Israel struck tents outside two major hospitals in the Gaza Strip overnight, killing at least two people, including a local reporter, and wounding nine, including six journalists, Palestinian medics said. Separate strikes killed at least 15 others across the Gaza Strip, according to hospitals.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Thousands of Ukrainian civilians are still held by Russia with uncertain hope of release
item.description: When she heard her front door open almost two years ago, Kostiantyn Zinovkin’s mother thought her son had returned home because he forgot something. Instead, men in balaclavas burst into the apartment in Melitopol, a southern Ukrainian city occupied by Russian forces.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas terror outlet quietly cuts Gaza death count, reveals most killed were combat-age men
item.description: New analysis shows thousands of names were removed from the Gaza casualty list as Hamas terror organization admits most deaths aged between 13 to 55 were males.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine will send a team to the US next week for talks on a new draft mineral deal
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine will send a team to Washington next week to begin negotiations on a new draft of a deal that would give the U.S. access to Ukraine’s valuable mineral resources, Economy Minister Yuliia Svyrydenko told The Associated Press. “The new draft agreement from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: JD Vance's warning on Europe's future shines spotlight on continent's growing list of problems
item.description: Many countries in Europe are struggling to come to terms with mass migration, rising crime and weak economies. But some nations have taken action and are seeing hopeful results.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China slaps a 34% tax on all US imports in retaliation for Trump's tariffs
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Friday that it will impose a 34% tax on all U.S. imports next week, part of a flurry of retaliatory measures to U.S. President Donald Trump’s new tariffs that delivered the strongest response yet from Beijing to the American leader's trade war.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, ELAINE KURTENBACH and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White House defends not including Russia, North Korea on tariffs
item.description: The official said that President Trump has “recently threatened to impose strong sanctions on Russia” to further explain leaving out Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation
